basics.description:
## 🛡️ SpyCloud Sentinel Supreme

![SpyCloud](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL/main/docs/images/SpyCloud-Logo-white.png)

**Transform recaptured darknet data into automated identity threat protection.**

This wizard deploys the complete SpyCloud integration including:
- CCF Data Connector with 5 REST API pollers
- 6 custom log tables (118+ columns)
- 5 Logic App playbooks (MDE, CA, Credential, Blocklist, TI Enrichment)
- 43+ analytics rules + Fusion + 5 Microsoft Security Incident Creation rules
- UEBA, Anomaly Detection, EyesOn audit settings
- 3 automation rules (auto-triage, device tasks, auto-close)
- 15 hunting queries across all MITRE ATT&CK tactics
- 4 watchlists (VIP, IOC Blocklist, Approved Domains, High-Value Assets)
- Security Copilot plugin + interactive agent (58+ skills)
- Comprehensive connector + feature recommendations

**Prerequisite:** SpyCloud Enterprise API key from [portal.spycloud.com](https://portal.spycloud.com)

[Documentation](https://github.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL) · [Architecture](https://github.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL/blob/main/docs/architecture.md)

options.text: This wizard guides you through every configuration option. Required fields are marked with *. All playbooks and analytics rules are optional and can be toggled individually.

In Azure, the Log Analytics workspace and Sentinel workspace are the SAME resource. Microsoft Sentinel is a solution installed ON TOP of a Log Analytics workspace. When you select or create a workspace here, ALL SpyCloud resources (DCE, DCR, tables, connector, playbooks, rules, workbook) will be deployed into that workspace. If creating new, Sentinel will be automatically enabled.

elements.name: createNewWorkspace
elements.label: Workspace Mode
elements.toolTip: Create a new Log Analytics workspace with Microsoft Sentinel enabled, or use an existing workspace that already has Sentinel.

allowedValues.label: Use existing workspace (Sentinel must be enabled)

elements.name: existingWorkspaceSelector
elements.label: Select Existing Workspace
elements.toolTip: Select an existing Log Analytics workspace that has Microsoft Sentinel enabled.

elements.name: newWorkspaceName
elements.label: New Workspace Name
elements.toolTip: Name for the new workspace. Must be 3-63 characters: letters, numbers, hyphens. Starts with letter.

constraints.validationMessage: 3-63 chars: letters, numbers, hyphens. Must start with a letter.

elements.name: retentionInDays
elements.label: Data Retention (days)
elements.toolTip: How long to retain data in Log Analytics. 90 days is included free. Longer retention incurs additional cost.

options.text: Each playbook is a Logic App with a system-assigned managed identity. API permissions (MDE Machine.Isolate, Graph User.ReadWrite.All) are assigned by the automated post-deploy script or can be granted manually in Azure Portal → Enterprise Applications.

elements.name: enableMdePlaybook
elements.label: Playbook 1: MDE Device Isolation (incident-triggered)
elements.toolTip: Searches MDE for infected devices, isolates them, tags with SpyCloud identifier, logs to audit table. Requires: MDE license + Machine.Isolate permission.

elements.name: mdeIsolationType
elements.label: MDE Isolation Type
elements.toolTip: Full: Completely disconnects device from network (except MDE agent). Selective: Limits connections but allows some traffic.

elements.name: mdeTagName
elements.label: MDE Device Tag
elements.toolTip: Tag applied to isolated devices in MDE for tracking and filtering.

elements.name: enableCaPlaybook
elements.label: Playbook 2: Conditional Access Identity Protection (incident-triggered)
elements.toolTip: Resets passwords, revokes sessions, adds users to CA exclusion group. Requires: Entra ID P1+ and User.ReadWrite.All.

elements.name: caSecurityGroupId
elements.label: CA Security Group Object ID (optional)
elements.toolTip: Entra ID security group Object ID for the Conditional Access exclusion group. Compromised users are added to this group to trigger CA policies (e.g., block sign-in, require MFA). Leave blank to skip group assignment.

elements.name: enableCredResponsePlaybook
elements.label: Playbook 3: Credential Response + Teams Alert (incident-triggered)
elements.toolTip: Checks recent sign-ins, forces password reset, revokes sessions, sends real-time Teams MessageCard to SOC. Requires: Security Administrator + Graph IdentityRisk.ReadWrite.All.

elements.name: enableMdeBlocklistPlaybook
elements.label: Playbook 4: MDE Blocklist — Scheduled Severity 25 Scan
elements.toolTip: Runs on a schedule to scan severity 25 (CRITICAL) infostealer records against MDE device inventory. Auto-isolates matched devices. Requires: MDE with API enabled.

elements.name: mdeBlocklistScheduleHours
elements.label: MDE Blocklist Scan Frequency
elements.toolTip: How often Playbook 4 scans for new severity 25 matches.

elements.name: enableAnalyticsRule
elements.label: Create primary infostealer detection rule
elements.toolTip: Creates the primary analytics rule that detects severity 20+ exposures and generates Sentinel incidents.

elements.name: enableAutomationRule
elements.label: Create automation rule (auto-trigger playbooks on incidents)
elements.toolTip: Creates an automation rule that fires the enabled playbooks when SpyCloud incidents are created.

elements.name: enableAnalyticsRulesLibrary
elements.label: Deploy FULL analytics rules library (22 rules, ALL disabled by default)
elements.toolTip: Deploys all 22 SpyCloud detection rules: plaintext passwords, PII exposure, session cookies, device re-infection, multi-domain exposure, geographic anomaly, executive/VIP, password reuse, remediation gaps, IdP correlation (Okta/Duo/Ping/Entra), and more. Every rule deploys DISABLED — you review and enable individually.

elements.name: enablePostDeployScript
elements.label: Run automated post-deployment script (resolves DCE/DCR + assigns RBAC)
elements.toolTip: Runs a deployment script inside the ARM deployment that waits for content template resources, resolves DCE Logs Ingestion URI and DCR Immutable ID, and assigns Monitoring Metrics Publisher to all Logic Apps. Eliminates manual post-deploy steps for core setup.

options.text: The SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Dashboard is a Sentinel Workbook with real-time charts and tables. It deploys directly into your Sentinel workspace and is accessible from Sentinel → Workbooks.

elements.name: enableWorkbook
elements.label: Deploy SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Dashboard (Sentinel Workbook)
elements.toolTip:
Deploys a comprehensive dashboard workbook with 10 visualizations:

• Exposure Overview tiles (total, unique users, devices, plaintext passwords)
• Exposure Trend over time by severity (timechart)
• Severity Distribution (pie chart)
• Top 20 Most Exposed Users (sortable table with severity heatmap)
• Password Type Distribution (bar chart)
• Top Targeted Domains (bar chart)
• Top 20 Infected Devices (table)
• Infections by Country (bar chart)
• Remediation Dashboard (MDE + CA action counts, remediation rate)
• Breach Catalog entries (table)

options.text: Tags are applied to ALL deployed resources. Default tags (solution, version, deployedBy) are always included. Add custom tags below for cost tracking, ownership, or compliance.

elements.name: tagEnvironment
elements.label: Environment Tag
elements.toolTip: Environment classification for all deployed resources.

elements.name: tagOwner
elements.label: Owner Tag
elements.toolTip: Team or individual responsible for these resources.

elements.name: tagCostCenter
elements.label: Cost Center Tag
elements.toolTip: Cost center for billing allocation.

elements.name: teamsChannelWebhook
elements.label: Microsoft Teams Webhook URL (optional)
elements.toolTip: Incoming webhook for your SOC Teams channel. Used by the Credential Response playbook for real-time alerts. Teams → Channel → Connectors → Incoming Webhook → Configure.

elements.name: serviceNowInstance
elements.label: ServiceNow Instance URL (optional)
elements.toolTip: ServiceNow instance for automatic ticket creation. Leave blank if not using ServiceNow.

elements.name: slackWebhookUrl
elements.label: Slack Incoming Webhook URL (optional)
elements.toolTip: Incoming Webhook URL for Slack SOC channel notifications. The Credential Response playbook sends alerts here. Create at: api.slack.com/apps → Incoming Webhooks.

elements.name: jiraWebhookUrl
elements.label: Jira Automation Webhook URL (optional)
elements.toolTip: Creates Jira issues for credential exposures via Automation webhook.

elements.name: azureDevOpsWebhookUrl
elements.label: Azure DevOps Service Hook URL (optional)
elements.toolTip: Creates work items in Azure DevOps via Service Hook.

elements.name: enableNotifications
elements.label: Enable email/Teams alert notifications
elements.toolTip: Creates an Action Group and health alert that notifies when SpyCloud data ingestion stops for 3+ hours.

elements.name: notificationEmail
elements.label: Notification Email
elements.toolTip: Email for alert notifications.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid email or leave blank.

elements.name: teamsWebhookUrl
elements.label: Teams Webhook for Health Alerts (optional)
elements.toolTip: Teams webhook for the health monitoring Action Group. Different from the SOC webhook above — this is for infrastructure alerts.

options.text: Slack webhook (configured in SOC Integrations above) also receives health alerts when the connector stops ingesting data.

These rules detect compromised SpyCloud credentials in your identity provider sign-in logs. Requires the IdP connector installed from Sentinel Content Hub.

• Okta: Content Hub → 'Okta Single Sign-On'
• Duo: Content Hub → 'Cisco Duo Security'
• Ping: Syslog/API via AMA agent
• Entra ID: Diagnostic Settings → SignInLogs

elements.name: enableSessionCookieDetection
elements.label: Enable session cookie / token theft detection
elements.toolTip: Rules for severity 25 exposures — stolen browser cookies and session tokens that allow MFA bypass.

elements.name: enableIdentityProviderAlerts
elements.label: Enable identity provider correlation rules (Okta/Duo/Ping/Entra)
elements.toolTip: Deploys 4 rules that cross-reference SpyCloud exposure data with IdP sign-in logs.

options.text:
These settings enable core Sentinel platform features that maximize SpyCloud threat detection. All are included in Microsoft Sentinel at no additional license cost.

--- UEBA (User and Entity Behavior Analytics) ---
Creates behavioral baselines for every user and entity. When SpyCloud detects credential exposure, UEBA anomalies reveal if those credentials are actively being exploited. Enables:
  - EntityAnalytics: Profiles users from Entra ID (identity provider)
  - Ueba: Processes SigninLogs, AuditLogs, AzureActivity, SecurityEvent
  - EyesOn: Audit trail of analyst activity in Sentinel
  - Required role: Security Administrator (on the workspace)
  - SpyCloud rule dependency: Rule #29 (UEBA Anomalous Behavior from Exposed User)

--- Anomaly Detection ---
ML-powered rules that detect deviations from normal behavior patterns:
  - Anomalous sign-in locations and times
  - Unusual resource access patterns
  - Abnormal data transfer volumes
  - Complements SpyCloud by detecting exploitation of exposed credentials
  - No additional configuration — rules auto-tune to your environment

--- Fusion Advanced Multistage Attack Detection ---
Patented ML that correlates low-fidelity alerts across kill chain stages:
  - Detects: credential theft -> lateral movement -> data exfiltration chains
  - Sources: All SpyCloud scheduled rules + Defender XDR + Entra ID Protection + Defender for Cloud + MCAS + Palo Alto
  - Output: High-confidence, low-volume incidents (typically 1-5 per week)
  - Note: Replaced by Defender XDR correlation engine if onboarded to unified Defender portal

elements.name: enableUEBA
elements.label: Enable UEBA (User and Entity Behavior Analytics)
elements.toolTip: Deploys 3 settings resources: EntityAnalytics (Entra ID provider), Ueba (SigninLogs, AuditLogs, AzureActivity, SecurityEvent data sources), and EyesOn (analyst audit tracking). Required role: Security Administrator. No additional license cost.

elements.name: enableAnomalies
elements.label: Enable Anomaly Detection (ML-powered behavioral anomalies)
elements.toolTip: Enables built-in ML anomaly detection rules. Auto-tunes to your environment over 14 days. Detects unusual sign-in patterns, resource access anomalies, and behavioral deviations. No additional license cost.

elements.name: enableFusionRule
elements.label: Enable Fusion Advanced Multistage Attack Detection
elements.toolTip: Deploys the Fusion analytics rule (template ID: f71aba3d-28fb-450b-b192-4e76a83015c8). Correlates alerts from: SpyCloud scheduled rules, Defender XDR, Entra ID Protection, Defender for Cloud, MCAS, Defender for Identity, and Palo Alto. No additional license cost.

elements.name: enableMicrosoftSecurityIncidentRules
elements.label: Create Microsoft Security Incident Creation rules (5 rules)
elements.toolTip:
Deploys 5 MicrosoftSecurityIncidentCreation rules that auto-create Sentinel incidents from alerts generated by:
1. Microsoft Defender XDR (Defender for Endpoint, Identity, Office 365, Cloud Apps)
2. Entra ID Protection (risky users, risky sign-ins)
3. Microsoft Defender for Cloud (VM, SQL, Storage, K8s, App Service)
4. Microsoft Cloud App Security (impossible travel, OAuth abuse, mass downloads)
5. Microsoft Defender for Identity (pass-the-hash, golden ticket, DCSync)

These ensure ALL Microsoft security alerts flow into Sentinel for cross-correlation with SpyCloud exposure data. Each rule requires the corresponding connector to be installed.

options.text:
The TI Enrichment playbook (Logic App) triggers on SpyCloud incident creation and automatically:

1. Extracts IP address entities from the incident
2. Queries VirusTotal API (v3) for IP reputation — malicious/suspicious/harmless scores
3. Queries AbuseIPDB API for abuse confidence score and total reports
4. Adds enrichment results as incident comments for SOC analyst review

Requirements:
  - VirusTotal API key (free tier: 4 requests/minute, 500/day — sufficient for most SOCs)
  - AbuseIPDB API key (optional — edit playbook post-deployment to add)
  - Logic App Contributor role on the resource group
  - Microsoft Sentinel Responder role for incident comment creation

Get your free VirusTotal API key: https://www.virustotal.com/gui/my-apikey
Get your free AbuseIPDB API key: https://www.abuseipdb.com/account/api

elements.name: enableTiEnrichmentPlaybook
elements.label: Deploy TI Enrichment Playbook (VirusTotal + AbuseIPDB auto-lookup)
elements.toolTip: Deploys a Logic App that automatically enriches SpyCloud incidents with threat intelligence from VirusTotal and AbuseIPDB. Runs on every incident creation. VirusTotal free tier: 4 req/min, 500/day.

elements.name: virusTotalApiKey
elements.toolTip: Get your free API key at: https://www.virustotal.com/gui/my-apikey — Copy the 64-character hex string from your profile page.

options.text:
VALIDATE THESE PREREQUISITES BEFORE DEPLOYING. Incomplete prerequisites will cause deployment failures or limited functionality.

=== REQUIRED FOR ALL DEPLOYMENTS ===

[1] SpyCloud Enterprise API Key
    - Obtain from: https://portal.spycloud.com > Account Settings > API Keys
    - Test your key: curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' -H 'X-Api-Key: YOUR_KEY' https://api.spycloud.io/enterprise-v2/breach/catalog/1
    - Expected: HTTP 200. If 401: wrong key or wrong tier. Contact support@spycloud.com
    - Enterprise tier: Watchlist + Breach Catalog pollers
    - Enterprise+ tier: Adds Compass Data + Compass Devices pollers

[2] Azure RBAC Roles (on the target resource group)
    Verify with: az role assignment list --assignee $(az ad signed-in-user show --query id -o tsv) --scope /subscriptions/{sub}/resourceGroups/{rg} --query '[].roleDefinitionName' -o tsv

    REQUIRED:
    - Microsoft Sentinel Contributor — create/manage connectors, analytics rules, watchlists, hunting queries
    - Log Analytics Contributor — create tables, manage DCR/DCE, write data
    - Monitoring Metrics Publisher — required on the DCR for data ingestion via DCE

    REQUIRED IF ENABLING PLAYBOOKS:
    - Logic App Contributor — create and manage Logic App workflows
    - Managed Identity Operator — assign managed identity to Logic Apps
    - Microsoft Sentinel Automation Contributor — connect automation rules to playbooks

    REQUIRED IF ENABLING UEBA:
    - Security Administrator — configure EntityAnalytics and UEBA settings

[3] Network Requirements
    - Outbound HTTPS (443) from Azure to: api.spycloud.io
    - No inbound rules required — all connections are outbound-only
    - If using Private Link: ensure DCE is accessible from the workspace
    - If using Firewall/Proxy: whitelist api.spycloud.io and *.spycloud.com

[4] Log Analytics Workspace
    - Existing workspace with Microsoft Sentinel enabled, OR
    - Template can create a new workspace (toggle in Step 1)
    - Pricing tier: PerGB2018 (pay-as-you-go) recommended for most deployments
    - Commitment tier: 100GB/day+ for large enterprises (significant discount)

=== WHAT WORKS WITHOUT EXTRA LICENSES (Microsoft Sentinel only) ===

- All 5 SpyCloud data pollers (CCF connector)
- All 6 custom log tables (156 columns total)
- All 44 analytics rules (38 scheduled + 1 Fusion + 5 MSIC)
- All 15 hunting queries
- All 4 watchlists (VIP, IOC Blocklist, Approved Domains, High-Value Assets)
- All 4 automation rules
- Workbook dashboard (19+ visualizations)
- UEBA, Anomaly Detection, EyesOn, Fusion
- 6 notification channels (Slack, Teams, Email, ServiceNow, Jira, Azure DevOps)
- Security Copilot plugin + agent (requires Copilot license separately)

=== PLAYBOOK LICENSE REQUIREMENTS ===

Playbook 1: MDE Device Isolation
  License: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2 (P2)
  Cost: ~$5.20/user/month (included in Microsoft 365 E5)
  Enables: Automatic device isolation, security tagging, device info lookup
  API Permissions: Machine.Isolate, Machine.ReadWrite.All (WindowsDefenderATP)
  Without license: Playbook deploys but isolation actions return 403 Forbidden
  Purchase: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/business/endpoint-security/microsoft-defender-endpoint

Playbook 2: Conditional Access Identity Protection
  License: Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2
  Cost: P1 ~$6/user/month (included in M365 E3), P2 ~$9/user/month (included in M365 E5)
  Enables: Force password reset, revoke sessions, add user to CA group
  API Permissions: User.ReadWrite.All, Directory.ReadWrite.All, GroupMember.ReadWrite.All (Microsoft Graph)
  Without P1+: Password reset and CA group assignment fail. Session revocation may work.
  Purchase: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/business/microsoft-entra-pricing

Playbook 3: Credential Response
  License: Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2 (for password reset)
  Enables: UPN-based targeted password reset + Teams/Slack/Email notifications
  Without license: Notifications work, password reset fails

Playbook 4: MDE Blocklist
  License: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Plan 2 (P2)
  Enables: Block malicious IPs/domains from SpyCloud infostealer data via MDE custom indicators
  API Permissions: Ti.ReadWrite (WindowsDefenderATP)
  Without license: Scheduled scans run but blocklist updates return 403

Playbook 5: TI Enrichment
  License: None required (uses free-tier external APIs)
  Requires: VirusTotal API key (free: 4 req/min) + optional AbuseIPDB key (free: 1000 req/day)
  Enables: Automatic IP reputation lookup on incident creation

=== MICROSOFT SECURITY PRODUCT LICENSES (for MSIC rules) ===

MSIC Rule: Defender XDR
  License: Microsoft 365 E5, E5 Security, or standalone Defender plans
  Without: Rule deploys but no alerts flow (no source data)

MSIC Rule: Entra ID Protection
  License: Microsoft Entra ID P2 (included in M365 E5)
  Without: Rule deploys but no risky user/sign-in detections

MSIC Rule: Defender for Cloud
  License: Microsoft Defender for Cloud (per-resource pricing varies)
  Without: Rule deploys but no Defender for Cloud alerts

MSIC Rule: Cloud App Security
  License: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (included in M365 E5)
  Without: Rule deploys but no MCAS alerts

MSIC Rule: Defender for Identity
  License: Microsoft Defender for Identity (~$5.50/user/month, included in M365 E5)
  Without: Rule deploys but no on-prem AD threat detections

=== SECURITY COPILOT ===

  License: Microsoft Security Copilot (consumption-based, ~$4/SCU/hour)
  Enables: 58+ natural language investigation skills, autonomous agent triage
  Without: Plugin and agent YAML files still deploy but cannot be used
  Purchase: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/business/ai-machine-learning/microsoft-security-copilot

Run these commands in Azure Cloud Shell (bash) to validate permissions BEFORE deploying:

--- CHECK YOUR CURRENT ROLES ---
az role assignment list --assignee $(az ad signed-in-user show --query id -o tsv) --query '[].{Role:roleDefinitionName, Scope:scope}' -o table

--- ASSIGN MISSING ROLES (requires Owner/User Access Administrator) ---

# Core deployment roles:
SUB_ID=$(az account show --query id -o tsv)
RG_NAME="your-resource-group"
USER_ID=$(az ad signed-in-user show --query id -o tsv)

az role assignment create --assignee $USER_ID --role 'Microsoft Sentinel Contributor' --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/resourceGroups/$RG_NAME
az role assignment create --assignee $USER_ID --role 'Log Analytics Contributor' --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/resourceGroups/$RG_NAME
az role assignment create --assignee $USER_ID --role 'Logic App Contributor' --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/resourceGroups/$RG_NAME

# UEBA (if enabling):
az role assignment create --assignee $USER_ID --role 'Security Administrator' --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/resourceGroups/$RG_NAME

--- POST-DEPLOYMENT: GRANT PLAYBOOK PERMISSIONS ---
After deployment, run scripts/grant-permissions.sh from Cloud Shell to:
  1. Assign MDE API permissions (Machine.Isolate, Machine.ReadWrite.All)
  2. Assign Graph API permissions (User.ReadWrite.All, Directory.ReadWrite.All, GroupMember.ReadWrite.All)
  3. Assign Monitoring Metrics Publisher on DCR
  4. Grant Sentinel Automation Contributor to playbook managed identities

Or manually:
  Azure Portal > Enterprise Applications > [Logic App name] > Permissions > Grant admin consent

--- VERIFY SENTINEL IS ENABLED ---
az sentinel onboarding-state show --resource-group $RG_NAME --workspace-name your-workspace

--- VERIFY DATA CONNECTOR HEALTH ---
az sentinel data-connector list --resource-group $RG_NAME --workspace-name your-workspace --query '[].{Name:name, Kind:kind}' -o table

elements.name: sectionTier1
elements.label: TIER 1: CRITICAL Connectors (Required for Core SpyCloud Rules)

options.text:
These connectors are NOT deployed by this template but are REQUIRED for maximum SpyCloud analytics rule coverage. Without these, 20+ analytics rules will have no source data to correlate against.

=== 1. Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) ===
Priority: CRITICAL
Cost: FREE (included with any Azure subscription)
SpyCloud Rules Enabled: #6 (Exposed User Sign-In), #7 (Risky Sign-In After Exposure), #10 (MFA Bypass), #18 (Impossible Travel), #29 (UEBA Anomaly), plus 7 hunting queries
Tables: SigninLogs, AuditLogs, AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs, AADManagedIdentitySignInLogs

Step-by-step setup:
  1. Azure Portal > Microsoft Entra ID > Diagnostic settings > Add diagnostic setting
  2. Name: 'sentinel-entra-logs'
  3. CHECK: SignInLogs, AuditLogs, NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs, ManagedIdentitySignInLogs, ServicePrincipalSignInLogs, RiskyUsers, UserRiskEvents
  4. Destination: Send to Log Analytics workspace > select your Sentinel workspace
  5. Save
  6. Verify: Run in Log Analytics: SigninLogs | take 10
  7. First data appears within 5-10 minutes

Alternative (Content Hub): Sentinel > Content Hub > search 'Microsoft Entra ID' > Install > Data Connectors > Microsoft Entra ID > Open connector page > Enable desired log types

Required role: Global Administrator or Security Administrator in Entra ID
Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/reports-monitoring/howto-integrate-activity-logs-with-log-analytics

=== 2. Microsoft Defender XDR (Microsoft 365 Defender) ===
Priority: CRITICAL
Cost: Requires Microsoft 365 E5, E5 Security, or standalone Defender plans
SpyCloud Rules Enabled: #11 (MDE Device Correlation), #12 (MDE Alert + Exposure), #13 (MDE Network Correlation), Fusion source, plus lateral movement hunting query
Tables: DeviceInfo, DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceLogonEvents, AlertEvidence, AlertInfo, IdentityLogonEvents, IdentityDirectoryEvents, EmailEvents, CloudAppEvents

Step-by-step setup:
  1. Sentinel > Content Hub > search 'Microsoft Defender XDR' > Install
  2. Data Connectors > Microsoft Defender XDR > Open connector page
  3. Under 'Connect incidents and alerts': Toggle ON
  4. Under 'Connect entities': Select ALL available event types
  5. Click 'Apply Changes'
  6. Verify: Run in Log Analytics: DeviceInfo | take 10
  7. Historical data backfill available (up to 30 days)

Required role: Global Administrator or Security Administrator
Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/connect-microsoft-365-defender

=== 3. Azure Activity ===
Priority: CRITICAL
Cost: FREE (included with any Azure subscription)
SpyCloud Rules Enabled: #2 (Exposed User Cloud Admin Activity), UEBA data source
Tables: AzureActivity

Step-by-step setup:
  1. Sentinel > Data Connectors > Azure Activity > Open connector page
  2. Click 'Launch Azure Policy Assignment Wizard'
  3. Scope: Select your subscription(s)
  4. Parameters: Primary Log Analytics workspace = your Sentinel workspace
  5. Remediation: Check 'Create a remediation task'
  6. Review + Create
  7. Verify: Run in Log Analytics: AzureActivity | take 10
  8. Data appears within 5-15 minutes

Required role: Owner or Contributor on the subscription
Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/data-connectors/azure-activity

=== 4. Office 365 ===
Priority: CRITICAL
Cost: FREE (included with Microsoft 365 subscription)
SpyCloud Rules Enabled: #14 (Email Compromise After Exposure), mailbox compromise hunting query, data exfiltration hunting query
Tables: OfficeActivity

Step-by-step setup:
  1. Sentinel > Data Connectors > Office 365 > Open connector page
  2. CHECK: Exchange, SharePoint, Teams
  3. Click 'Apply Changes'
  4. Verify: Run in Log Analytics: OfficeActivity | take 10

Required role: Global Administrator or Security Administrator
Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/data-connectors/office-365

These connectors enable advanced cross-correlation between SpyCloud exposure data and Microsoft security products. Each adds significant detection coverage.

=== 5. Microsoft Defender for Cloud ===
Cost: Per-resource pricing (varies by workload type)
SpyCloud MSIC Rule: Auto-creates incidents from Defender for Cloud alerts. Fusion source.
Tables: SecurityAlert, SecurityRecommendation

Setup:
  1. Defender for Cloud > Environment Settings > select subscription > Enable desired plans
  2. Continuous Export > Add > Export to Log Analytics workspace > select Sentinel workspace
  3. Export: Security alerts (all severities), Security recommendations
  4. Save
Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/defender-for-cloud/continuous-export

=== 6. Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (MCAS) ===
Cost: Included in Microsoft 365 E5, or standalone ~$3.50/user/month
SpyCloud MSIC Rule: Auto-creates incidents from MCAS alerts. Detects impossible travel, OAuth abuse, mass file downloads by compromised users.
Tables: CloudAppEvents, McasShadowItReporting

Setup:
  1. Content Hub > search 'Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps' > Install
  2. Data Connectors > Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps > Open connector page
  3. Enable alerts and discovery logs
  4. Apply Changes
Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/data-connectors/microsoft-defender-for-cloud-apps

=== 7. Microsoft Defender for Identity ===
Cost: ~$5.50/user/month (included in Microsoft 365 E5)
SpyCloud MSIC Rule: Auto-creates incidents from Defender for Identity alerts. Critical for detecting pass-the-hash, golden ticket, DCSync, reconnaissance from stolen on-prem AD credentials.
Tables: IdentityLogonEvents, IdentityDirectoryEvents, IdentityQueryEvents

Setup:
  1. Content Hub > search 'Microsoft Defender for Identity' > Install
  2. Deploy sensor on domain controllers: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/defender-for-identity/install-sensor
  3. Data Connectors > Microsoft Defender for Identity > Enable
Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/data-connectors/microsoft-defender-for-identity

=== 8. DNS Analytics ===
Cost: FREE (Windows DNS) or included with DNS server
SpyCloud Rules Enabled: #3 (DNS + SpyCloud Infected IP Correlation)
Tables: DnsEvents, DnsInventory

Setup (Windows DNS via AMA):
  1. Content Hub > search 'DNS' > Install
  2. Deploy Azure Monitor Agent on DNS servers
  3. Create Data Collection Rule targeting DNS event logs
Setup (third-party DNS): Forward DNS logs via Syslog/CEF to AMA
Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/data-connectors/dns

=== 9. Threat Intelligence ===
Cost: FREE (TAXII feeds) or per Microsoft Defender TI license
SpyCloud Rules Enabled: #17 (SpyCloud + TI Cross-Reference), Fusion source, TI enrichment
Tables: ThreatIntelligenceIndicator

Setup (TAXII 2.0/2.1):
  1. Data Connectors > Threat Intelligence - TAXII > Open connector page
  2. Enter: Friendly name, API root URL, Collection ID, Username/Password
  3. Popular free TAXII feeds: Anomali Limo, FS-ISAC (requires membership), AlienVault OTX
  4. Click Add
Setup (Microsoft Defender TI):
  1. Content Hub > search 'Microsoft Defender Threat Intelligence' > Install
  2. Data Connectors > Microsoft Defender Threat Intelligence > Enable
Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/connect-threat-intelligence-taxii

These connectors extend SpyCloud detection to third-party identity providers, firewalls, EDR, and multi-cloud environments.

=== IDENTITY PROVIDERS ===

10. Okta Single Sign-On
  Cost: Requires Okta subscription + Okta System Log API access
  SpyCloud Rule: #15 (Okta Sign-In from Exposed User)
  Table: Okta_CL
  Setup: Content Hub > 'Okta Single Sign-On' > Install > Configure API token from Okta Admin Console > Security > API > Tokens
  Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/data-connectors/okta-single-sign-on

11. Cisco Duo Security
  Cost: Requires Duo subscription + Admin API access
  SpyCloud Rule: #4 (Duo Auth from Exposed User)
  Table: DuoSecurityAuthentication_CL
  Setup: Content Hub > 'Cisco Duo Security' > Install > Configure integration key, secret key, API hostname from Duo Admin Panel > Applications > Admin API
  Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/data-connectors/cisco-duo-security

12. Ping Identity / PingOne
  Cost: Requires Ping subscription + API access
  SpyCloud Rule: #16 (Ping Auth from Exposed User)
  Table: PingFederateAudit_CL
  Setup: Configure Syslog/API forwarding via AMA agent to Log Analytics workspace

=== FIREWALLS & NETWORK ===

13. Palo Alto Networks (PAN-OS)
  Cost: Requires Palo Alto firewall subscription
  SpyCloud Rule: #32 (Firewall + SpyCloud Infected IP), Fusion raw log source
  Table: CommonSecurityLog
  Setup: Content Hub > 'Palo Alto Networks' > Install > Configure CEF/Syslog forwarding via AMA
  Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/data-connectors/palo-alto-networks

14. Fortinet FortiGate
  Cost: Requires Fortinet subscription
  Table: CommonSecurityLog
  Setup: Content Hub > 'Fortinet FortiGate' > Install > Configure CEF forwarding
  Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/data-connectors/fortinet-fortigate

15. Zscaler Internet Access
  Cost: Requires Zscaler subscription
  Table: CommonSecurityLog
  Setup: Content Hub > 'Zscaler' > Install > Configure NSS feed to CEF

=== EDR & ENDPOINT ===

16. CrowdStrike Falcon
  Cost: Requires CrowdStrike subscription
  Table: CommonSecurityLog or CrowdStrike_CL
  Setup: Content Hub > 'CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection' > Install > Configure Falcon Data Replicator or API connector

=== MULTI-CLOUD ===

17. AWS CloudTrail
  Cost: AWS CloudTrail free tier (management events)
  Table: AWSCloudTrail
  Setup: Content Hub > 'Amazon Web Services' > Install > Configure S3 bucket + SQS queue
  Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/connect-aws

18. Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
  Cost: GCP audit logging (included)
  Table: GCPAuditLogs
  Setup: Content Hub > 'Google Cloud Platform' > Install > Configure Pub/Sub topic
  Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/connect-google-cloud-platform

=== FEATURES TO ENABLE AFTER DEPLOYMENT ===

1. Security Copilot Plugin + Agent
  WHERE: Security Copilot portal > Settings > Custom Plugins > Upload
  FILES: copilot/SpyCloud_Plugin.yaml (58+ KQL skills), copilot/SpyCloud_Agent.yaml (autonomous investigation)
  REQUIRES: Microsoft Security Copilot license (consumption-based)
  SKILLS: Credential investigation, breach metadata, device forensics, Compass intelligence, remediation tracking, cross-table correlation, UEBA correlation, Fusion analysis, TI enrichment, malware family tracking, executive summaries

2. Sentinel Repositories (CI/CD)
  WHERE: Sentinel > Settings > Repositories > Connect
  HOW: Connect your GitHub or Azure DevOps repository for version control of analytics rules, playbooks, and hunting queries
  BENEFIT: Automated deployment of rule updates, audit trail, rollback capability
  Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/ci-cd

3. Watchlist Population
  After deployment, populate these watchlists with your organization's data:
  - SpyCloud-VIP-Watchlist: Add executive/admin email addresses for priority alerting
  - SpyCloud-IOC-Blocklist: Add known malicious IPs/domains from your threat feeds
  - SpyCloud-Approved-Domains: Add all your corporate email domains
  - SpyCloud-HighValue-Assets: Add critical servers, DCs, VPN gateways, database servers
  WHERE: Sentinel > Watchlists > select watchlist > Edit

4. Analytics Rule Review
  All 38 scheduled SpyCloud rules deploy DISABLED by default. Review and enable:
  WHERE: Sentinel > Analytics > filter by 'SpyCloud' > select each rule > Enable
  RECOMMENDATION: Enable rules in phases — start with severity 20+ infostealer rules, then expand

5. Automation Rule Tuning
  - Auto-Escalate Critical: Enabled by default — auto-promotes plaintext/cookie/MFA bypass incidents to High severity
  - Auto-Task Device Infections: Enabled by default — adds investigation tasks for device incidents
  - Auto-Close Informational: DISABLED by default — enable after reviewing incident volume
  WHERE: Sentinel > Automation > Automation Rules

6. Summary Rules (Cost Optimization)
  For high-volume environments (100K+ records/day), consider creating summary rules:
  WHERE: Azure Monitor > Summary Rules > Create
  USE CASE: Aggregate SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL data by domain/severity for faster dashboard queries
  Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sentinel/summary-rules

7. Data Retention Optimization
  Configure per-table retention to balance cost and compliance:
  WHERE: Log Analytics > Tables > select table > Manage table > Data retention
  RECOMMENDATION:
    - SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL: 90-365 days (depends on remediation SLA)
    - SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL: 365-730 days (reference data, low volume)
    - Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL: 90 days (audit trail)
    - SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL: 90 days (audit trail)
    - SpyCloudCompassData_CL: 90-180 days (moderate volume)
    - SpyCloudCompassDevices_CL: 90-180 days (low volume)

SpyCloud uses a tiered security group architecture for Conditional Access enforcement. Create these groups in Entra ID BEFORE deployment, then enter their Object IDs below. Playbooks will automatically move users between groups based on exposure severity.

| Group | Purpose | CA Policy to Create |
|-------|---------|--------------------|
| SpyCloud-Blocked | Severity 25 — block all access | Block access from all locations |
| SpyCloud-Restricted | Severity 20 — restricted access | Require compliant device + MFA |
| SpyCloud-Monitored | Severity 5 — enhanced monitoring | Require MFA from untrusted locations |
| SpyCloud-Remediated | Post-remediation tracking | Normal access (monitoring only) |

To create groups: Entra ID > Groups > New Group > Security > enter name > Create

elements.name: blockedGroupId
elements.label: SpyCloud-Blocked Group Object ID
elements.toolTip: Object ID of the 'SpyCloud-Blocked' security group. Users with severity 25 exposures are added here. Create a CA policy that blocks all access for this group.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid GUID (e.g., 12345678-abcd-1234-efgh-123456789012) or leave blank.

elements.name: restrictedGroupId
elements.label: SpyCloud-Restricted Group Object ID
elements.toolTip: Object ID of the 'SpyCloud-Restricted' security group. Users with severity 20 exposures are added here. Create a CA policy that requires compliant device + MFA for this group.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid GUID or leave blank.

elements.name: monitoredGroupId
elements.label: SpyCloud-Monitored Group Object ID
elements.toolTip: Object ID of the 'SpyCloud-Monitored' security group. Users with severity 5 exposures are added here.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid GUID or leave blank.

options.text: These settings control how playbooks manage authentication methods for compromised users. Disabling auth methods forces users to re-register, which improves security but creates friction. Test with a small group before enabling organization-wide.

elements.name: enableMfaReRegistration
elements.label: Force MFA re-registration for severity 25 exposures
elements.toolTip: When enabled, the playbook will delete all existing MFA registrations for users with severity 25 (critical) exposures, forcing them to re-register. This prevents attackers from using compromised MFA tokens.

elements.name: disableWeakAuthMethods
elements.label: Disable SMS/phone MFA for severity 20+ exposures
elements.toolTip: When enabled, the playbook will disable SMS and phone call MFA methods for exposed users, requiring them to use Microsoft Authenticator or FIDO2 security keys instead.

elements.name: enforceFido2Only
elements.label: Enforce FIDO2/hardware token only for severity 25 exposures
elements.toolTip: Most restrictive option. Disables ALL auth methods except FIDO2 security keys and certificate-based authentication (CBA). Only enable if your organization has FIDO2 keys deployed.

options.text: Configure how SpyCloud playbooks respond to infected devices detected in the exposure data. Actions are severity-based and fully customizable.

elements.name: enableAutoIsolation
elements.label: Auto-isolate devices for severity 25 exposures
elements.toolTip: Automatically isolate devices in MDE when SpyCloud detects severity 25 (critical) infostealer infections. Requires MDE P2.

elements.name: enableIntuneCompliance
elements.label: Mark devices non-compliant in Intune for severity 20+
elements.toolTip: Mark infected devices as non-compliant in Intune, triggering Conditional Access device compliance policies.

elements.name: deviceAutoReleaseHours
elements.label: Auto-release isolation after (hours)
elements.toolTip: Automatically release device isolation after this many hours. Set to 'Never' to require manual release.

options.text: Enable firewall integrations to automatically push SpyCloud IOCs (IP addresses, domains) to your perimeter firewalls. Supports Fortinet FortiGate, Palo Alto Networks, and Azure Firewall. Requires API access to your firewall management plane.

elements.name: enableFortinetIntegration
elements.label: Enable Fortinet FortiGate integration
elements.toolTip: Push SpyCloud IPs to FortiGate address objects and correlate with FSSO sessions.

elements.name: fortinetApiUrl
elements.label: FortiGate Management URL
elements.toolTip: HTTPS URL for FortiGate REST API. Must be accessible from Azure Logic Apps.

elements.name: enablePaloAltoIntegration
elements.label: Enable Palo Alto Networks integration
elements.toolTip: Push SpyCloud IPs to PAN-OS address groups and correlate with User-ID traffic.

elements.name: paloAltoApiUrl
elements.label: PAN-OS Management URL
elements.toolTip: HTTPS URL for PAN-OS XML API. Must be accessible from Azure Logic Apps.

elements.name: enableAzureFirewall
elements.label: Enable Azure Firewall IP group updates
elements.toolTip: Automatically update Azure Firewall IP groups with SpyCloud compromised IPs.

options.text:
=== CONNECTOR TROUBLESHOOTING ===

PROBLEM: No data after 15 minutes
CAUSE: API key invalid, wrong tier, or network issue
FIX: Test your key: curl -H 'X-Api-Key: YOUR_KEY' https://api.spycloud.io/enterprise-v2/breach/catalog/1
  - 200 response = key is valid
  - 401 response = key is invalid or expired
  - Connection timeout = network/firewall blocking outbound HTTPS to api.spycloud.io

PROBLEM: 401 Unauthorized on connect
CAUSE: Wrong API key type (SaaS vs Enterprise)
FIX: Ensure you have an ENTERPRISE tier key from portal.spycloud.com > Account Settings > API Keys

PROBLEM: Watchlist data appears but Catalog is empty
CAUSE: API permissions or endpoint access
FIX: Contact SpyCloud support — Catalog may require separate endpoint authorization

PROBLEM: Compass tables are empty
CAUSE: Enterprise+ subscription required
FIX: Compass Data and Compass Devices require Enterprise+ tier. Contact SpyCloud sales to upgrade

PROBLEM: Data appears but stops after initial sync
CAUSE: API rate limiting or cursor/checkpoint issue
FIX: Check DCR metrics in Azure Monitor > Data Collection Rules. If rate limited, increase polling interval to 60min+

PROBLEM: Duplicate records appearing
CAUSE: Overlapping lookback period during initial sync
FIX: Normal behavior — deduplicate in KQL using: | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by document_id

PROBLEM: High ingestion costs
CAUSE: Large watchlist with all severity levels
FIX: 1) Filter to severity >= 20 (infostealer only) 2) Increase polling interval 3) Reduce retention 4) Use summary rules

=== PLAYBOOK TROUBLESHOOTING ===

PROBLEM: Playbooks are not triggering on incidents
CAUSE 1: Analytics rule is disabled
FIX: Sentinel > Analytics > filter 'SpyCloud' > verify rules are Enabled
CAUSE 2: Automation rule is disabled
FIX: Sentinel > Automation > verify SpyCloud automation rules are Enabled
CAUSE 3: Logic App is disabled
FIX: Resource Group > Logic Apps > verify each SpyCloud Logic App shows 'Enabled'

PROBLEM: Playbook fails with 403 Forbidden
CAUSE: Managed identity missing required permissions
FIX: Run scripts/grant-permissions.sh from Cloud Shell, OR manually assign:
  - MDE Playbook: Machine.Isolate, Machine.ReadWrite.All (on WindowsDefenderATP)
  - CA Playbook: User.ReadWrite.All, Directory.ReadWrite.All, UserAuthenticationMethod.ReadWrite.All (on Microsoft Graph)
  - Enrichment Playbook: SecurityEvents.Read.All (on Microsoft Graph)

PROBLEM: MDE isolation fails
CAUSE: No Defender for Endpoint P2 license or device not onboarded
FIX: Verify MDE P2 license. Check device is onboarded: security.microsoft.com > Device inventory

PROBLEM: Password reset fails
CAUSE: Missing Entra ID P1+ license or insufficient permissions
FIX: Verify Entra P1+. Assign User.ReadWrite.All to Logic App managed identity

PROBLEM: MFA re-registration fails
CAUSE: Missing UserAuthenticationMethod.ReadWrite.All permission
FIX: Assign via PowerShell:
  Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All'
  $sp = Get-MgServicePrincipal -Filter "displayName eq 'SpyCloud-CA-Remediation-{workspace}'"
  # Assign UserAuthenticationMethod.ReadWrite.All

PROBLEM: Security group assignment fails
CAUSE: Missing Directory.ReadWrite.All permission
FIX: Assign Directory.ReadWrite.All to Logic App managed identity

PROBLEM: Teams notification not sending
CAUSE: Invalid webhook URL or Teams connector expired
FIX: Teams > Channel > Connectors > Incoming Webhook > regenerate URL > update Logic App parameter

PROBLEM: ServiceNow ticket creation fails
CAUSE: Invalid instance URL or authentication
FIX: Verify instance URL format (https://instance.service-now.com), check API credentials

TIP: Check Logic App Run History
  Resource Group > Logic Apps > select playbook > Run History > click failed run > expand actions to see error details

=== ANALYTICS RULE TROUBLESHOOTING ===

PROBLEM: Rule shows 'Error' status in Sentinel
CAUSE: KQL syntax error or missing table
FIX: Open rule > Edit > Run query > check for errors in Log Analytics

PROBLEM: Rule creates no incidents despite having data
CAUSE: Threshold too high or time range mismatch
FIX: Open rule > Edit > lower triggerThreshold or expand queryPeriod

PROBLEM: Too many incidents being created
CAUSE: Threshold too low for your data volume
FIX: Increase triggerThreshold, add severity filters, or group incidents by entity

PROBLEM: Cross-source correlation rules show no results
CAUSE: Required data source not connected
FIX: Install the required connector from Content Hub:
  - SigninLogs rules: Install 'Microsoft Entra ID' connector + enable Sign-in Logs diagnostic
  - OfficeActivity rules: Install 'Office 365' connector
  - CommonSecurityLog rules: Install your firewall vendor's connector (Fortinet, PaloAlto, etc.)
  - BehaviorAnalytics rules: Enable UEBA (Sentinel > Settings > UEBA > ON)
  - DeviceEvents rules: Install 'Microsoft Defender for Endpoint' connector
  - DnsEvents rules: Install 'DNS' connector + enable DNS Analytics

PROBLEM: UEBA correlation rules show no results
CAUSE: UEBA not enabled or not enough data for baseline
FIX: Enable UEBA (Sentinel > Settings > UEBA > ON). UEBA needs 14+ days to build baselines

PROBLEM: Fusion rule not detecting multi-stage attacks
CAUSE: Fusion disabled or insufficient correlated signals
FIX: Enable Fusion (Sentinel > Analytics > filter 'Fusion' > Enable). Fusion needs multiple signal sources

TIP: Test any rule query manually in Log Analytics before enabling

=== DIAGNOSTIC QUERIES (run in Log Analytics) ===

1. OVERALL HEALTH CHECK:
union
  (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Watchlist'),
  (SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Catalog'),
  (SpyCloudCompassData_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Compass Data'),
  (SpyCloudCompassDevices_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Compass Devices')
| project Table, Records, Latest

2. CONNECTOR HEALTH:
SentinelHealth
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where SentinelResourceType == 'Data connector'
| where SentinelResourceName contains 'SpyCloud'
| project TimeGenerated, Status, Description

3. INGESTION VOLUME (last 7 days):
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize DailyRecords=count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| render timechart

4. PLAYBOOK EXECUTION STATUS:
SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL
| union Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize Actions=count(), Successes=countif(ActionStatus=='Success'), Failures=countif(ActionStatus=='Failed') by Action

5. SEVERITY DISTRIBUTION:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| summarize Count=count() by severity
| order by severity

6. TOP EXPOSED USERS:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| summarize Exposures=count(), MaxSeverity=max(severity) by email
| order by Exposures desc
| take 20

7. REMEDIATION GAP (exposures without CA action > 48h):
let exposedUsers = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(48h) and severity >= 20
| distinct email;
let remediatedUsers = SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(48h)
| distinct Email;
exposedUsers
| where email !in (remediatedUsers)

=== PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION ===

1. POLLING INTERVAL
  - Start with 30min (recommended for most orgs)
  - Reduce to 5-15min for active incident response
  - Increase to 60min+ for cost-sensitive environments

2. SEVERITY FILTERING
  - Start with ALL severity levels to understand your exposure landscape
  - After 1 week, if volume is overwhelming, filter to severity >= 20 (infostealer only)
  - Enable severity 2-5 (breach) rules only when you have capacity to triage

3. DATA RETENTION OPTIMIZATION
  Per-table recommendations:
  - SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL: 90d (active remediation) to 365d (compliance)
  - SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL: 730d (reference data, very low volume)
  - SpyCloudCompassData_CL: 90-180d (moderate volume)
  - SpyCloudCompassDevices_CL: 90-180d (low volume)
  - Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL: 90d (audit trail)
  - SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL: 90d (audit trail)

4. SUMMARY RULES (for high-volume environments)
  If ingesting 100K+ records/day:
  - Create summary rules to pre-aggregate by domain, severity, and date
  - Use summary tables for workbook/dashboard queries
  - Keep detailed tables for investigation and hunting only

5. ANALYTICS RULE PHASING
  Week 1: Enable core infostealer detection (severity 20+)
  Week 2: Enable device infection and session cookie rules
  Week 3: Enable O365 and Entra ID correlation rules
  Week 4: Enable UEBA, firewall, and DNS correlation rules

6. WORKBOOK OPTIMIZATION
  - Always use time range parameters (default 24h for live, 30d for trends)
  - Use summarize/project in KQL to minimize data scanned
  - Consider pinning key panels to Azure Dashboard for quick access

=== SUPPORT CONTACTS ===
- SpyCloud API: support@spycloud.com
- GitHub Issues: github.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL/issues
- Microsoft Sentinel: Azure Portal > Support + Troubleshooting
- Playbook Permissions: Run scripts/grant-permissions.sh from Cloud Shell
